content:
# Scale Formation

**Summary**: The precipitation of minerals like iron sulfide that can block pores.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2021-gasimli|Gasimli, 2021]] investigated the formation and inhibition of iron sulfide scale in water injection wells.

- [[2023-othman|Othman, 2023]] addressed scale-formation by using chelating agents to sequester ions.

## Scientific Context

- [[2021-gasimli|Gasimli, 2021]] A major cause of formation damage and production loss.

- [[2023-othman|Othman, 2023]] addressed scale-formation by using chelating agents to sequester ions.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2021-gasimli|Gasimli, 2021]] Coreflooding experiments

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Iron sulfide scale significantly damages core permeability, with one sample showing 68% loss. ([[2021-gasimli|Gasimli, 2021, p. 145]])
- [[2021-gasimli|Gasimli, 2021]] 68% permeability reduction

- Calcium sulfate and barium sulfate are the most problematic scales in reservoirs when using seawater. ([[2023-othman|Othman, 2023, p. 53]])
